title: ARCA Orchestration Proposal (ADR-089)
_comment: Contract for the Orchestration Proposal that @architect-ai emits on /orchestrate. The structural schema (properties) documents the shape; x-node-type-gate-floor encodes the Layer-1 critic floor that validate-orchestration-proposal.sh enforces. Same declarative shape as hooks/lib/phase-gates.json blocking[], generalized from fixed-cycle to per-node. Updates here are pipeline policy and require an ADR.

template_recommendation._comment: If != none, the planner ADVISES using the fixed pipeline (/ml-new, /htb-new, /redteam-new) instead of this bespoke DAG. Borrowed-from-C idea (ADR-089): dynamic mode never re-derives a battle-tested flow.

compound_handoff._comment: true -> the DAG trips the compound decision matrix (>2 coordinated LLM calls / verifier-in-loop / multi-provider) -> @architect-ai hands design to @compound-ai-architect.

blocking_gates._comment: Adversarial co-agents / critics that gate THIS node's output. Validator rejects if the floor (x-node-type-gate-floor) is under-declared.

is_terminal_closer._comment: Marks the terminal node of the DAG. The runtime hook dynamic-orchestration-gate-enforcer.sh treats it as an additional phase-closer, so a code-producing DAG that does NOT end on chief-architect/deployment still trips the code-critic gate at runtime. Closes the gap noted in ADR-089.

produces_deployable._comment: Node emits a deployable / serving artifact -> floor requires code-critic + chief-architect downstream.

hits_adversarial_signals._comment: Node invokes an LLM in a way that hits the 7 adversarial signals (public endpoint, auth, PII, prod LLM, unvalidated input, file upload, external integration) -> floor requires ai-red-teamer.

status._comment: NO node executes until status == APPROVED. Schema-mandated approval gate (Hard Constraint #2d).

x-node-type-gate-floor._comment: Layer-1 critic floor. validate-orchestration-proposal.sh rejects any proposal where a node under-declares these gates. required_gates_in_order is an ORDERED SUBSEQUENCE check against the node's blocking_gates[]: the gates must appear in this relative order (gaps allowed). Math-producers are also code-producers but get the stricter chain; the validator applies the FIRST matching category.

global_rules._comment_terminal: If ANY node is a math/code producer, the proposal MUST declare >=1 node with is_terminal_closer:true. This is what lets the runtime hook close the gap.
